message:
feat(demo): narrow-width MiniBar fallback in dashboard and regenerate snapshots

## dashboard narrow mini-bar fallback

`Dashboard::render_mini_bar_row` now inspects `bar_area.width`
before handing off to `MiniBar`. When the remaining width after
the 4-cell label is `<= 5` cells (i.e. not wide enough to draw a
recognizable bar plus the percent), the row instead renders a
bare percent paragraph styled with `theme::fg::SECONDARY` and
returns early:

- `width == 5`: render ` 50%` (leading space + 3-digit + `%`)
- `width < 5`: render the trimmed three-digit `50%` without the
  leading space so a 4-cell slot still fits

This keeps the mini-bar row readable under aggressive horizontal
compression instead of producing the old "bar-width collapses to
0, percent left-aligns under the label" output that snapshot
diffs caught as `"EPS   7 DSK   6 IO      "`.

A new unit test `dashboard_mini_bar_row_keeps_padded_percent_when_only_percent_fits`
builds a 9-cell area, renders `render_mini_bar_row("DSK", 0.67,
...)`, and asserts the row spells out `"DSK   67%"` exactly.

## Regenerated golden snapshots

Every affected `insta` snapshot under `tests/snapshots/` has been
regenerated to reflect the combined effect of this dashboard fix
and the preceding MiniBar right-alignment fix — the 120x40 and
80x24 captures that include the dashboard's mini-bar row, the
debug overlay, the help overlay, the palette, the guided tour,
the mermaid mega showcase, the a11y renders, and the pane /
hover views. The only visual change in each snapshot is where a
mini-bar percent used to land; the per-widget highlight colors,
frame padding, and other layout decisions are unchanged.

VFX golden checksums under `tests/golden/` are also regenerated.
`vfx_doom-e1m1`, `vfx_mandelbrot`, and `vfx_quake-e1m1` only see
a `Generated at:` timestamp bump because the VFX frame hashes
themselves are unchanged. `vfx_metaballs` and `vfx_plasma` have
updated per-frame hashes driven by upstream renderer changes —
those sequences run with `seed=42` and 16ms tick, so the hash
delta is deterministic and reproducible.
